---
title: "Consensus Trees: SNaQ and Findgraphs"
format: html
engine: julia
---

# Purpose

Build per-setting consensus networks across the 100 replicate per-replicate
networks produced by SNaQ and find_graphs, and summarize edge support, hybrid
placement, and bipartition recovery.

**Inputs:**
- `output/<paramname>/rep<NN>/snaqfolder/H{0,1}_output/H{0,1}.out` (per-rep SNaQ nets)
- `output/<paramname>/rep<NN>/findgraph/rep<NN>_admix{0,1}_unique_graphs.rds`

**Outputs (written under `consensus_trees/`):**
- `<method>_consensus_trees/per_setting/*_H{0,1}{.nwk,_edge_support.csv,_plot.pdf}`
- `<method>_consensus_trees/by_{dup,rv,sf}/...` (aggregated views)

**Notes:**
- This analysis is supplementary and not part of the main paper's headline
  results. Kept in the repository for completeness.
- If `RCall` fails to load on your machine because of a libstdc++ ABI
  mismatch, set `LIBSTDCXX_PATH` to a compatible libstdc++.so.6 before
  rendering (see the `Libdl.dlopen` block in Setup).

## Setup
```{julia}
#| output: false
# Load system libstdc++ before RCall so Rcpp.so finds CXXABI_1.3.15.
# On systems where RCall fails with "version `CXXABI_1.3.15' not found",
# set the environment variable LIBSTDCXX_PATH to your libstdc++.so.6 (e.g.
# from a recent conda env or a recent gcc install). Otherwise this block
# is a no-op and Julia/RCall use the system loader as usual.
using Libdl
if haskey(ENV, "LIBSTDCXX_PATH") && isfile(ENV["LIBSTDCXX_PATH"])
    Libdl.dlopen(ENV["LIBSTDCXX_PATH"], Libdl.RTLD_GLOBAL)
end
using PhyloNetworks, PhyloSummaries, PhyloPlots, RCall
using DataFrames, CSV

include("../scripts/utilities.jl")
include("../third_party_scripts/interop_admixtools.jl")

const OUTGROUP = "A"
const N_REPS = 100

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers: load per-rep networks
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

function load_snaq_h0_netsample(outfolder, n_reps=N_REPS)
    nets = HybridNetwork[]
    for rep in 1:n_reps
        repstr = lpad(rep, ndigits(n_reps), '0')
        f = joinpath(outfolder, "rep$repstr", "snaqfolder", "H0_output", "H0.out")
        isfile(f) && push!(nets, readnewick(f))
    end
    nets
end

function load_snaq_h1_netsample(outfolder, n_reps=N_REPS)
    nets = HybridNetwork[]
    for rep in 1:n_reps
        repstr = lpad(rep, ndigits(n_reps), '0')
        f = joinpath(outfolder, "rep$repstr", "snaqfolder", "H1_output", "H1.out")
        isfile(f) && push!(nets, readnewick(f))
    end
    nets
end

function load_findgraphs_h0_netsample(outfolder, n_reps=N_REPS)
    R"library(admixtools)"
    nets = HybridNetwork[]
    for rep in 1:n_reps
        repstr = lpad(rep, ndigits(n_reps), '0')
        rds = joinpath(outfolder, "rep$repstr", "findgraph",
                       "rep$(repstr)_admix0_unique_graphs.rds")
        !isfile(rds) && continue
        graphs = R"readRDS($rds)"
        n_graphs = rcopy(Int, R"length($graphs)")
        n_graphs == 0 && continue
        el = try rcopy(R"as.data.frame($graphs[[1]]$graph)") catch; continue end
        net = try edgelist_to_net(el) catch; continue end
        push!(nets, net)
    end
    nets
end

function load_findgraphs_h1_netsample(outfolder, n_reps=N_REPS)
    R"library(admixtools)"
    nets = HybridNetwork[]
    for rep in 1:n_reps
        repstr = lpad(rep, ndigits(n_reps), '0')
        rds = joinpath(outfolder, "rep$repstr", "findgraph",
                       "rep$(repstr)_admix1_unique_graphs.rds")
        !isfile(rds) && continue
        graphs = R"readRDS($rds)"
        n_graphs = rcopy(Int, R"length($graphs)")
        n_graphs == 0 && continue
        el = try rcopy(R"as.data.frame($graphs[[1]]$graph)") catch; continue end
        net = try edgelist_to_net(el) catch; continue end
        push!(nets, net)
    end
    nets
end

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers: load saved consensus files
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

"""Load consensus H0 tree and edge-support CSV. Returns (net, esup_df) or (nothing, nothing)."""
function load_consensus_h0(prefix)
    nwk = "$(prefix).nwk"
    isfile(nwk) || return nothing, nothing
    net = readnewick(nwk)
    csv = "$(prefix)_edge_support.csv"
    esup = isfile(csv) ? CSV.read(csv, DataFrame) : nothing
    return net, esup
end

"""Load saved consensus level-1 H1 network, restoring node numbers. Returns net or nothing."""
function load_consensus_h1(prefix)
    nwk = "$(prefix)_net.nwk"
    isfile(nwk) || return nothing
    net = readnewick(nwk)
    resetnodenumbers_fromnames!(net)
    net
end

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers: plot functions
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

"""Two-panel plot: H0 consensus tree (plain + edge-support labelled)."""
function plot_h0_consensus(net, esup, title_str; pdf_path=nothing)
    pdf_path === nothing || R"pdf($pdf_path, width=9, height=5)"
    R"par"(mar=[0, 0, 1.5, 0])
    if esup !== nothing
        R"layout"([1 2])
        rootatnode!(net, OUTGROUP)
        plot(net)
        R"mtext"("H0 consensus tree", side=3, line=0)
        ecol = Dict(r[:number] => (r[:support] < 70 ? "orange2" : "black")
                    for r in eachrow(esup))
        plot(net, edgelabel=esup, edgecolor=ecol)
        R"mtext"("H0 edge support (% reps)", side=3, line=0)
    else
        R"layout"(1)
        rootatnode!(net, OUTGROUP)
        plot(net)
        R"mtext"("H0 consensus tree – $title_str", side=3, line=0)
    end
    R"mtext"(title_str, outer=true, side=3, line=-1)
    pdf_path === nothing || R"dev.off()"
end

"""Three-panel hybrid-sisters support plot for H1 consensus network."""
function plot_h1_hybrid_support(net, netsample, title_str; pdf_path=nothing)
    isempty(netsample) && return
    BSe_majortree, _ = treeedges_support(netsample, net)
    BSn, BSe, _, _, _ = hybridclades_support(netsample, net)

    pdf_path === nothing || R"pdf($pdf_path, width=12, height=5)"
    R"layout"([1 2 3])
    R"par"(mar=[1.5, 0, 1.5, 0])

    plot(net, edgelabel=BSe_majortree)
    R"mtext"("support for edges to be in major tree", side=3, line=-1)
    R"mtext"("support are in %, not proportions!", side=1, line=-1)

    plot(net, edgelabeladj=[.5, -.2], nodelabeladj=[.5, .5],
         edgelabelcolor="orangered", nodelabelcolor="deepskyblue4",
         edgelabel=select(BSe, [:edge, :BS_hybrid_edge]),
         nodelabel=select(BSn, [:hybridnode, :BS_hybrid_samesisters]))
    R"mtext"("support for sister-hybrid relationships", side=3, line=-1)
    R"mtext"("blue: hybrid + both sisters", side=1, line=-1)

    hyb_rows = filter(row -> row[:BS_hybrid] > 0, BSn)
    if nrow(hyb_rows) > 0
        plot(net, edgelabeladj=[.5, -.2],
             edgelabel=hyb_rows[!, [:edge, :BS_hybrid]])
    else
        plot(net)
    end
    R"mtext"("support for hybrid clades", side=3, line=-1)

    R"mtext"(title_str, outer=true, side=3, line=-1)
    pdf_path === nothing || R"dev.off()"
end
```

```{julia}
outroot = joinpath(@__DIR__, "..", "output")
param_folders = sort(filter(isdir,
    [joinpath(outroot, d) for d in readdir(outroot)]))
@show length(param_folders)
```

```{julia}
#| output: false

"""Parse the `dup`, `rv`, `n_ind`, `sf` fields from an output folder name."""
function parse_paramname(paramname::String)
    m = match(r"DUP([\d.]+)-LOS[\d.]+-RV([A-Z]+)-N_ind(\d+)-SF([\d.]+)", paramname)
    m === nothing && error("Cannot parse paramname: $paramname")
    (dup=String(m[1]), rv=String(m[2]), n_ind=String(m[3]), sf=String(m[4]))
end

"""
    group_folders(param_folders, key)
Group output folders by a parsed field: `key` ∈ {:dup, :rv, :n_ind, :sf}.
Returns `Dict{String,Vector{String}}` mapping group value → folder list.
"""
function group_folders(param_folders::Vector{String}, key::Symbol)
    groups = Dict{String,Vector{String}}()
    for f in param_folders
        val = getfield(parse_paramname(basename(f)), key)
        push!(get!(groups, val, String[]), f)
    end
    groups
end

"""
    build_consensus(outfolders, load_h0, load_h1, out_dir, label)
Load all H0/H1 networks from `outfolders`, compute greedy consensus (H0) and
consensus level-1 network (H1), then save `.nwk`, CSVs, and PDFs under `out_dir`.
`label` is used as the filename stem and plot title.
"""
function build_consensus(outfolders::Vector{String}, load_h0, load_h1,
                         out_dir::String, label::String)
    mkpath(out_dir)
    safe   = replace(label, r"[^A-Za-z0-9_.\-]" => "_")
    pfx_h0 = joinpath(out_dir, "$(safe)_H0")
    pfx_h1 = joinpath(out_dir, "$(safe)_H1")

    # ── H0 greedy consensus ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    nets_h0 = vcat([load_h0(f) for f in outfolders]...)
    println("  H0: $(length(nets_h0)) trees  ($(length(outfolders)) settings)")
    if length(nets_h0) >= 2
        con_h0 = consensustree(nets_h0)
        rootatnode!(con_h0, OUTGROUP)
        writenewick(con_h0, pfx_h0 * ".nwk"; support=true)
        esup = DataFrame(
            number  = [e.number for e in con_h0.edge if !isexternal(e)],
            support = [round(100 * e.y, digits=1) for e in con_h0.edge if !isexternal(e)]
        )
        CSV.write(pfx_h0 * "_edge_support.csv", esup)
        plot_h0_consensus(con_h0, esup, "H0: $label"; pdf_path=pfx_h0 * "_plot.pdf")
        println("  H0 → $(pfx_h0)_plot.pdf")
    else
        @warn "H0 skipped (fewer than 2 trees): $label"
    end

    # ── H1 consensus level-1 network ────────────────────────────────────────
    nets_h1 = vcat([load_h1(f) for f in outfolders]...)
    println("  H1: $(length(nets_h1)) networks  ($(length(outfolders)) settings)")
    if length(nets_h1) >= 2
        res_h1 = consensus_level1network(nets_h1, outgroup=OUTGROUP, suppressinfo=true)
        consensus_level1network_save(res_h1, pfx_h1)
        plot_h1_hybrid_support(res_h1[:net], nets_h1, "H1: $label";
                               pdf_path=pfx_h1 * "_plot.pdf")
        println("  H1 → $(pfx_h1)_plot.pdf")
    else
        @warn "H1 skipped (fewer than 2 networks): $label"
    end
end

"""
    build_consensus_grouped(param_folders, key, base_out_dir, load_h0, load_h1)
Build one consensus per unique value of `key` ∈ {:dup, :rv, :n_ind, :sf},
saving to `base_out_dir/by_<key>/`.
"""
function build_consensus_grouped(param_folders::Vector{String}, key::Symbol,
                                  base_out_dir::String, load_h0, load_h1)
    groups  = group_folders(param_folders, key)
    out_dir = joinpath(base_out_dir, "by_$(key)")
    for (val, folders) in sort(collect(groups))
        label = "$(key)_$(val)"
        println("\n=== $label  ($(length(folders)) settings, ≤$(100*length(folders)) reps) ===")
        build_consensus(folders, load_h0, load_h1, out_dir, label)
    end
end
```


## Build SNaQ consensus trees (from raw replicates)
```{julia}
snaq_build_dir = joinpath(@__DIR__, "..", "consensus_trees", "snaq_consensus_trees")
```

### Level 1: per parameter setting (100 reps each)

```{julia}
for outfolder in param_folders
    paramname = basename(outfolder)
    println("\n=== $paramname ===")
    build_consensus([outfolder], load_snaq_h0_netsample, load_snaq_h1_netsample,
                    joinpath(snaq_build_dir, "per_setting"), paramname)
end
```

### Level 2: by rate variation (1200 reps each)

```{julia}
build_consensus_grouped(param_folders, :rv, snaq_build_dir,
                        load_snaq_h0_netsample, load_snaq_h1_netsample)
```

### Level 3: by dup/loss rate (1200 reps each)

```{julia}
build_consensus_grouped(param_folders, :dup, snaq_build_dir,
                        load_snaq_h0_netsample, load_snaq_h1_netsample)
```

### Level 4: by ILS / SF (1800 reps each)

```{julia}
build_consensus_grouped(param_folders, :sf, snaq_build_dir,
                        load_snaq_h0_netsample, load_snaq_h1_netsample)
```

### Level 5: by number of individuals (1800 reps each)

```{julia}
build_consensus_grouped(param_folders, :n_ind, snaq_build_dir,
                        load_snaq_h0_netsample, load_snaq_h1_netsample)
```

---

## Build Findgraphs consensus trees (from raw replicates)

```{julia}
R"library(admixtools)"
fg_build_dir = joinpath(@__DIR__, "..", "consensus_trees", "findgraphs_consensus_trees")
```

### Level 1: per parameter setting (100 reps each)

```{julia}
for outfolder in param_folders
    paramname = basename(outfolder)
    println("\n=== $paramname ===")
    build_consensus([outfolder], load_findgraphs_h0_netsample, load_findgraphs_h1_netsample,
                    joinpath(fg_build_dir, "per_setting"), paramname)
end
```

### Level 2: by rate variation (1200 reps each)

```{julia}
build_consensus_grouped(param_folders, :rv, fg_build_dir,
                        load_findgraphs_h0_netsample, load_findgraphs_h1_netsample)
```

### Level 3: by dup/loss rate (1200 reps each)

```{julia}
build_consensus_grouped(param_folders, :dup, fg_build_dir,
                        load_findgraphs_h0_netsample, load_findgraphs_h1_netsample)
```

### Level 4: by ILS / SF (1800 reps each)

```{julia}
build_consensus_grouped(param_folders, :sf, fg_build_dir,
                        load_findgraphs_h0_netsample, load_findgraphs_h1_netsample)
```

### Level 5: by number of individuals (1800 reps each)

```{julia}
build_consensus_grouped(param_folders, :n_ind, fg_build_dir,
                        load_findgraphs_h0_netsample, load_findgraphs_h1_netsample)
```
